title: Dungeon Universalis - Boxing it all Up
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wwtaQ_PNx1M

A short bonus video from Daniel (@dungeondive) addressing the most asked question after the three-part deep dive: does **Dungeon Universalis** all fit back in the box?

Yes. Even with every chit, standee and tile punched out, every component fits in the original box without any third-party trays. Two baggies cover most of it: one for small dungeon-feature tokens (mounts, doors, beds, staircases, exit/entrance markers), another for square corridor tiles and the dual player boards. Standees go in their own bag along with the longer tiles — Daniel slips those in to stop them sticking up and bending. Books and the campaign map sit flat on top, joined by his spiral-bound black-and-white personal copy of the rulebook (which is what he's actually using to learn the game).

The cardboard chit tray, all the standees sorted by size, the cards, the dice, and the coins all live inside the inner organiser. The only complaint: the box itself is a little flimsy for the weight, and Daniel has reinforced the corners. He notes that if the box were as hefty as Myth's, this would qualify as the ultimate package.

Praise to Ludic Dragon for designing storage that *works* without the player having to solve it.

----

Daniel's standard for in-box storage is high — he calls Myth-tier the gold standard. Which board games in your collection have actually delivered first-party storage you didn't need to replace?
